pattern: Track the two smallest values and their columns from the previous row; each cell in the current row can use whichever of the two minimums does not share its column, making the DP transition O(n) per row.
hints: For each row you need the minimum from the previous row that was NOT in the same column — storing the top two minimums and their columns lets you answer that in O(1) per cell.

After processing one row, extract the smallest value (and which column it came from) and the second-smallest value. For the next row, every column except the first minimum's column uses the first minimum; that column uses the second minimum.
Initialise a dp array as the first row, then iterate rows 1..n-1: find the best and second-best dp values, update each cell as grid[r][c] + (best if best_col != c else second_best).

edge_inputs.description: 2x2 grid, each row must pick from the other column
greedy column choice changes per row because a later row has a large value at the greedy column
3-row grid where the second-best of row 1 is needed

stress.description: large square grid with random values
